channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: EMEA Masters Winter Group Draw — Who Will Witchcraft Face?
item.content:encoded:
Riot Games has completed the EMEA Masters Winter 2026 Group Draw to decide the match-ups for the crucial Tier 2 League of Legends event.

The EMEA Regional League (ERL) tournament directly feeds into the new Esports World Cup qualifier. The top two (non-academy) teams at EMEA Masters Winter will compete with LEC teams for the chance to qualify for the big-money LoL tournament in Saudi Arabia.

The upcoming Group Stage follows on from the Champions Round, a new format which saw the top eight ranked teams compete for a direct bye into the Playoffs Upper Bracket, with Misa Esports, Verdant, Solary, and Movistar KOI Fénix all advancing. Meanwhile, LEC Versus participants Karmine Corp Blue and Witchcraft (formerly Los Ratones) directly qualified into the Group Stages despite not competing in an ERL this split.

The 32 Group Stage teams will compete in best-of-threes in their respective four-team groups. Each group will be double-elimination, meaning two losses will spell elimination from the competition.

Of the eight groups, only one winner from each will advance to the Playoffs for a chance to compete for the title.

EMEA Masters Winter 2026 Groups and Opening Matches

The groups and opening matches for EMEA Masters Winter are as follows:

Group A

  • Barça eSports
  • BRUTE
  • DMG Esports
  • Karmine Corp Blue

March 10, 12:00 EDT/16:00 GMT: BRUTE vs Barça

March 10, 12:00 EDT/16:00 GMT: Karmine Corp Blue vs DMG

Group B

  • Galions
  • Vantex Esports
  • UCAM Esports
  • Bushido Wildcats

March 10, 12:00 EDT/16:00 GMT: Bushido Wildcats vs UCAM

March 10, 15:00 EDT/19:00 GMT: Vantex vs Galions

Group C

  • Arctic Pandas
  • Colossal Gaming
  • Nightbirds
  • Team Heretics Academy

March 10, 12:00 EDT/16:00 GMT: Colossal Gaming vs Artic Pandas

March 10, 15:00 EDT/19:00 GMT: Team Heretics Academy vs Nightbirds

Group D

  • G2 NORD
  • Lupus Esports
  • Baam Esports
  • Witchcraft

March 10, 15:00 EDT/19:00 GMT: Lupus vs G2 NORD

March 10, 15:00 EDT/19:00 GMT: Witchcraft vs Baam

Group E

  • HMBLE
  • Barcząca Esports
  • French Flair
  • 3BL Esports

March 11, 12:00 EDT/16:00 GMT: Barcząca vs HMBLE

March 11, 15:00 EDT/19:00 GMT: 3BL vs French Flair

Group F

  • Berlin International Gaming
  • Otter Side
  • S2G Esports
  • Frites Esports Club

March 11, 12:00 EDT/16:00 GMT: Frite Esports Club vs S2G

March 11, 15:00 EDT/19:00 GMT: Otter Side vs BIG

Group G

  • WLGaming Esports
  • The Bandits
  • Unicorns of Love Sexy Edition
  • Partizan Sangal

March 11, 12:00 EDT/16:00 GMT: Partizan Sangal vs Unicorns of Love Sexy Edition

March 11, 15:00 EDT/19:00 GMT: The Bandits vs WLGaming

Group H

  • FN Esports
  • Team Phantasma
  • Bomba Team
  • Eintracht Spandau

March 11, 12:00 EDT/16:00 GMT: Team Phantasma vs FN Esports

March 11, 15:00 EDT/19:00 GMT: Entracht Spandau vs Bomba Team

How do you think Witchcraft will perform after a drama-filled start for the new team? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord.

And see who LEC champions G2 Esports will face in their first international match of 2026 with the LoL First Stand opening matches

MOBILE APP

item.title: Battlefield 6 Player Count Getting Slammed by Close Competitor on Steam
item.content:encoded:
Battlefield 6 continues to struggle amidst lacklustre updates and, recently, a wave of layoffs. The game, which was billed as a turning point for the popular FPS franchise, hit rocky terrain shortly after it launched in October 2025, and it has found it tough to gain any meaningful traction since.

The game’s player count is dropping quite consistently, and it was recently noted by some fans that it’s being beaten by one of its closest competitors. On Steam, Delta Force, which resembles Battlefield in many ways, had more than three times as many players as Battlefield 6, at the time of writing.

Delta Force On Top

When Delta Force was revealed (then known as Delta Force: Hawk Ops), it was labelled a ‘Battlefield clone’, and for good reason. It looked, felt, and played like Battlefield 2042, right down to the class system, UI, setting, and gameplay loop.

However, as time has gone on, Delta Force, which was released in early access in December 2024, has only gotten stronger. It recently underwent its eighth major content drop, and a steady supply of content, exciting extraction modes, and a solid, global player base have all contributed to the game’s growth.

At the time of writing, Delta Force boasted 122,480 players on Steam. Battlefield 6, on the other hand, had just 35,874. Even Marathon, the somewhat beleaguered extraction title from Bungie, was on the cusp of beating Battlefield 6, touted by many as being the revitalization of the age-old shooter series.

While Delta Force was sitting pretty in ninth place overall on Steam, Battlefield 6 occupied a space further down the board in 44th place, around three weeks after the game’s last major update.

Many are now concerned about the future of Battlefield 6, but what do you think? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Marathon’s player count woes have been addressed by other developers

Written by

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant's adoration for gaming started on the Amstrad CPC in 1996. Now, he's a multi-platform expert gamer with a deep love for the industry, a passion for esports, and more…

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Available on iOS & Android

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Marathon ‘Player Count Watching’ is ‘Unemployed Behavior’, Says Overwatch Dev
item.content:encoded:
Everyone is concerning themselves with Marathon’s player count, one way or another. Either you’re curious to see if the game will fail, or you’re hopeful it will succeed. In recent days, we’ve seen social media channels packed full of people talking about the impending collapse of Marathon, which isn’t as likely as you might think.

This conversation has prompted developers far and wide to share their thoughts about ‘player count watching’. As you can imagine, they’re not best pleased with the rhetoric.

Is Marathon Dying? Who Cares?

In a post on social media, one user was talking about a ‘50% player count drop’ on Marathon, suggesting that, just days after release, the extraction shooter from Bungie is facing the abyss and is about to fall in. That’s far from the truth just now, but who knows, maybe one day.

In response, a senior designer working on Overwatch, Dylan Snyder, took to social media with a damning statement:

Is the 50% player drop in the room with us right now?

Feel free to dislike and pass up any game you want, more power to you, but this is big unemployed, maidenless behavior…

The ‘behavior’ Snyder is talking about refers to the personality that some build, which results in them doing nothing but talking about the ebb and flow of games on social media. It’s often accompanied by little more than numbers screen-captured from SteamDB, as that’s typically the only platform with public figures.

Marathon has, at the time of writing, dropped to 33,645 players from an all-time high at launch of 88,337. It is showing a negative trend, but this was almost to be expected. Extraction shooters aren’t the most popular games in the world, and they’re not adopted as quickly or widely as other titles.

Marathon also has a premium paywall in front of it. It’s not extortionate, but it’s something. There’s also the inherent complexity of the game and the niche art style. There are a lot of things that might prevent the game from getting the sweeping and universal praise some are hoping for, but the numbers it has right now a) aren’t indicative of the game on all platforms, and b) might be just fine for Bungie.

Do you think it’s acceptable to bag on a game every time it sees a drop on SteamDB? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that GTA 6’s source code might be in the wild

Written by

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant's adoration for gaming started on the Amstrad CPC in 1996. Now, he's a multi-platform expert gamer with a deep love for the industry, a passion for esports, and more…

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: MLB The Show 26 Release Date Countdown & Exact Start Times
item.content:encoded:
It’s that time of year when San Diego Studios readies us all for the leading baseball game in the world. MLB The Show 26 is the latest game in the franchise, and the series is still without a doubt the major leagues.

If you thought that people were getting sick of hitting home runs, you can think again, as San Diego Studio is looking to keep its run going and not strike out. MLB The Show 26 is the newest annual release, and its release date is quite familiar.

When Is MLB The Show 26 Coming Out? Release Date, Explained

MLB The Show 26 officially comes out on March 17 for several of the main platforms on the market.

If this isn’t your first MLB The Show purchase, then you probably already recognize the pattern—MLB The Show 26 is following the usual March release window.

  • More Insider Gaming: MLB The Show 26 Adds New Trade Hub to Franchise Mode

A fun fact for you: Of the last 12 MLB releases (including this one), 10 of them have been in March!

It’s usually the go-to month for MLB The Show and has been since MLB 08.

MLB The Show 26 Release Times

The first time zone that gets MLB The Show 26 is PT, at 9 PM on March 17, whereas the majority of other regions will technically need to wait until March 18.

It feels apt that America gets to play MLB The Show 26 the earliest, and 9 PM means you can get in some swinging practice before bedtime.

  • More Insider Gaming: MLB The Show 26 Expands Road to The Show With Deeper Career

Insider Gaming has provided an in-depth table below, showing you MLB 26 The Show’s release dates and start times across the world. Let the team know if you want any adding to help you out.

With a few nice features announced for this year’s game, what are you most looking forward to about MLB The Show 26? Share your thoughts and feedback via the Insider Gaming Discord.

If you haven’t already, make sure to check out MLB The Show 26’s first gameplay trailer and feature details.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Helldivers 2 Entrenched Division Warbond Details Revealed
item.content:encoded:
Helldivers 2’s next premium Warbond is called Entrenched Division and will arrive on March 17, 2026.

Arrowhead Game Studios just released a major update for Helldivers 2, with the Machinery of Oppression update taking the battle to Cyberstan and introducing 3 new enemy types. Just a month later, the developers have revealed a new Premium Warbond, with fresh weapons, stratagems, armor sets, and more.

Helldivers 2 Entrenched Division Weapons and Stratagems

Getting straight into business, Helldivers 2 Entrenched Division Premium Warbond comes with a brand-new flamethrower, the B/FLAM-80 Cremator. It is a heavy support weapon that can spray a continuous column of flame thanks to its massive backpack fuel tank. Keeping the flame theme going, there’s also the SMG/FLAM-34 Stoker, a primary submachine gun with iron sights and an incineration attachment.

Then you have the A/GM-17 Gas Mortar Sentry, which is an automated turret that can fire gas shells. Players also receive the P-69 Veto secondary pistol, which comes with jet-assisted rounds. There’s also a new throwable, the G-48 Giga Grenade, which is a good choice when you are going for impact instead of range. Lastly, the CQC-73 Entrenchment Tool will be available as a secondary weapon.

Helldivers 2 Entrenched Division Cosmetics

Coming to cosmetics, Helldivers 2 Entrenched Division Premium Warbond adds some interesting Armours and Capes along with new emote patterns and more. The first armor is the CPG-48 Sapper, a medium armor that features several pouches and big pockets to store battlefield essentials. It also features Concussive Padding for explosion resistance and room for extra throwables. The Dignity of Toil cape will match perfectly with this armor.

Next up is the light armour, CPH-26 Commandant, which comes with its own Concussive Padding for explosive and gas damage resistance, along with reduced sidearm recoil. This armour set goes perfectly with the new Crest of the Unsullied cape and player card.

Regarding patterns in the Helldivers 2 Entrenched Division Premium Warbond, players get the Heritage Olive pattern with the Helldivers 2 Entrenched Division Warbond, which can be used on hellpods, FRV, Pelican, and exosuits. The new emote is called Diplomatic Handshake, allowing players to shake on it in the middle of the battlefield.

The details for the new Premium Warbond were revealed through PlayStation’s blog post.

In other news, the release date for Helldivers 2 movie has been revealed. Additionally, the game has reportedly sold more than 20 million copies. What are your thoughts on the Helldivers 2 Entrenched Division Premium Warbond? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Fortnite increasing the price of V-Bucks this month. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP
